Research has identified the plant's potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating wounds and infections. In traditional medicine, Mesadenella cuspidata is often used to prepare topical creams, ointments, and tinctures to aid in wound healing and to combat microbial infections.
taxonomy: Mesadenella cuspidata is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the subtribe Cranichideae.
